results.title: A Boy, a dog and a frog
results.creator_credit: Gary Templeton, John Sturner and Evergreen Films
results.brief_description: This film follows the adventures of a small boy and his dog as they spend a day trying to capture a playful frog at a nearby pond. The film is a delightful, non-verbal production that will provide amusement and enjoyment for audiences of all ages.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Gary Templeton, John Sturner and Evergreen Films

results.title: A Boy's life
results.brief_description: Although embracing vastly different styles and aesthetices, the three films comprising “A boy’s life” present an intriguingly lucid biography of developing sexuality - from childhood via puberty to college days. Using humour to explore painful issues of emerging identity, both ‘Dottie Gets Spanked’ (director, Todd Haynes) and ‘Trevor’ (director, Peggy Rajski) are remarkable in that they address one of the ultimate taboos by acknowledging that children can and do have an awareness of their own sexuality. The third short film, ‘A Friend of Dorothy’ this is the tale of a sexually confused young man in his first term at college. Each film rings with authenticity: they represent stories, experiences and emotions that many gay men will instantly recognise.

results.title: A Book for every child
results.creator_credit: Alastair Matheson, John Balcomb, Mallica Vanarathon and UNICEF
results.brief_description: Enormous book shortages plague nearly every emerging nation. The creative potential of teachers is being tapped in India, Thailand and Uganda, where they are writing, illustrating and even hand-producing books for their own class rooms. From them, children are also learning to make this most basic teaching tool. Narrated by Edward Kemp.

results.title: A Blow, a kiss
results.creator_credit: Rey Carlson, Helene Nicol, Rhonda Schepisi and Great Southern Films
results.brief_description: A 10 year old boy and his father help an injured man who has crashed his motorcycle. The boy is confused and deeply affected by the reactions of both his father and the father of the motorcyclist, in the face of this trauma. Based on a short story by Tim Winton.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Rey Carlson, Helene Nicol, Rhonda Schepisi and Great Southern Films

results.title: A Blonde in love = Loves of a blonde
results.creator_credit: Milos Forman, Rudolf Hajek and CBK Films
results.brief_description: This early film by Milos Forman tells the story of a young girl’s(Hana Brejchova) infatuation with a pianist (Vladimir Pucholt) with whom she has spent the night. She then leaves the provinces and goes to search for him in Prague. Forman uses this simple theme to present a realist and comic view of 1960’s Czechoslovakia, in which he examines young love, the generation gap, and the working of bureaucracy. His unflinchingview of the everyday gave the film a unique quality at the time, but he also observes his characters with warmth and affection, revealing their inner strength in the face of adversity. Besides finding humour in the everyday, he also draws on the traditions of silent comedy. Miroslav Ondricek’s (long time Forman collaborator on both his Czech and American features) images have a remarkable resonance and Forman’s cast of non-actors provide him with a marvellous gallery of faces. Co-written with Czech filmmaker Ivan Passer. In Czeck, with English sub-titles.

results.title: A Bird's eye view
results.creator_credit: Roger Whittaker, ABC-TV (Australia) and Roger Whittaker Films
results.brief_description: The Kookaburra and the Magpie are two of our national icons. Everyone recognises the Kookaburra by its laugh, but in ‘Kookaburra’, its fascinating social system is revealed as well as its expert fishing abilities. The Magpie has a beautiful call but can be aggressive and territorial. The complete life cycle of this common but fascinating bird is explored in ‘Magpie’.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Roger Whittaker, ABC-TV (Australia) and Roger Whittaker Films

results.title: 7 Days in September
results.creator_credit: Steven Rosenbaum and Broadcast News Networks
results.brief_description: This moving, if rudimentarily filmed documentary, is a collective account of ‘September 11, 2001’ and that day’s impact on a variety of New Yorkers. The filmmakers utilise an array of original footage shot over that week by professionals and by amateurs; they also interview an array of people who lived in New York at the time who describe their own responses and the unusual occurrences witnessed. The narrative focuses on the feel in New York over the week: the mood on the street, impromptu prayer sessions that were conducted, for example, as well as debates that raged between strangers; the clean up effort and the way that people pulled together, helping each other, making donations, giving blood, and providing emotional support. Whilst in no way an analysis of the calamity, the filmmakers eloquently capture the small stories. The work in a minor way, also addresses the role and the dilemma of any documentary filmmaker caught up in a tragedy - to film or not to film and at what stage does observation of a grand-scale catastrophe cross over into exploitation and opportunism.

results.title: 60 cycles [French version]
results.creator_credit: Jean-Claude Labrecque and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: The 11th. St Laurent long distance cycling race with participants from 13 countries covers 1500 miles of the Quebec countryside in 12 days. From a distance, the cyclists are a moving colour frieze against the picturesque landscape; closer we see the taut faces and straining limbs of the competitiors above the bright circle of their machines.

results.title: A Banker in paradise
results.creator_credit: Jack Gardiner, John Kingsford-Smith and Kingcroft Productions
results.brief_description: Produced for the Bank of NSW as part of its 150th anniversary celebrations. Shows the scenery and colourful life of Fiji and the activities of an officer providing banking services.

results.title: 60 cycles
results.creator_credit: Jean-Claude Labrecque, Jacques Bobet and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: The 11th St. Laurent long-distance cycle race; with participants from thirteen countries; and fifteen hundred miles of Quebec countryside to cover in twelve days. From a distance the cyclists are a moving colourful frieze against the picturesque landscape; closer, we see the taut faces and straining limbs of the competitors above the bright circles of their machines. (Awards: Moscow, London, Prague, New York, Cork)
